text.title: AWS Monitoring, Alerting, and Disaster Recovery Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Alarm fatigue; team mutes the channel; real outage missed.
antipattern.root-cause: Single SNS topic used for all severities.
antipattern.fix: Split into critical / warning / info topics; route critical to pager only.

antipattern.symptom: DR plan exists on paper; first real outage finds the restore procedure broken; RTO blown by 10x.
antipattern.root-cause: Quarterly drill skipped or watered down to paper walkthroughs.
antipattern.fix: Schedule quarterly chaos-engineering exercise that actually kills primary and restores in DR region.

antipattern.symptom: MTTR doubles because every multi-service trace requires log-grep correlation.
antipattern.root-cause: Tracing off because 'not free' — but cost is negligible vs MTTR savings.
antipattern.fix: Enable active tracing on prod Lambda + API Gateway. Sample 5% baseline + 100% on errors.

antipattern.symptom: Dashboard has 40 widgets; on-call cannot find the metric that matters during an incident.
antipattern.fix: Cap at 6-8 widgets per dashboard. Per-tier dashboards. 5-min period, 3-h window.
